Zhong Ji Longevity Science Group Limited ( (HK:0767) ) just unveiled an update.

Zhong Ji Longevity Science Group Limited has announced a further delay in sending a shareholder circular related to its planned acquisition of 25% of Asian Integrated Cell Laboratory Limited, a major and connected transaction that will be funded through the issuance of convertible notes under a specific mandate. The circular, which will include detailed information on the deal, independent advice, financial data on the target and notice of a special general meeting, is now expected to be dispatched by 30 April 2026, extending the timetable for shareholder review and approval of this strategic move.

The postponement reflects the company’s need for additional time to prepare and finalise certain required information, indicating that regulatory compliance and disclosure standards under Hong Kong’s Listing Rules are driving the revised schedule. While the delay may push back the execution of the acquisition and the related financing structure, it also suggests the board is prioritising thorough documentation to support independent assessment by shareholders and advisors, which could influence investor confidence and the pace of the group’s expansion in cell science services.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0767) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.47 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zhong Ji Longevity Science Group Limited stock, see the HK:0767 Stock Forecast page.

More about Zhong Ji Longevity Science Group Limited

Zhong Ji Longevity Science Group Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the longevity and life-science related sector. The group is expanding its presence in advanced biomedical services through investments such as its planned acquisition of a stake in Asian Integrated Cell Laboratory Limited, a cell science-focused business in the region.

Aluminum Corporation of China Limited has announced a sharp improvement in its preliminary first-quarter 2026 results, projecting net profit attributable to shareholders of between RMB5.30 billion and RMB5.59 billion, a year-on-year rise of 50% to 58%. Greentech Technology International Limited ( (HK:0195) ) has shared an announcement.

Greentech Technology International Limited has postponed its board meeting originally scheduled for 8 April 2026 to 15 April 2026, as the company requires additional time to finalize its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2024 and its unaudited interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2025. The rescheduled meeting is intended to consider and approve these delayed financial statements, underscoring ongoing reporting challenges.

Trading in Greentech Technology International shares has been suspended on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange since 2 September 2024 due to the delay in publishing the unaudited interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2024 and will remain halted until the company satisfies the resumption guidance set by the exchange. The prolonged suspension and repeated timetable revisions highlight continued transparency and compliance pressures for the company, with implications for investor confidence and regulatory oversight.

Analogue Holdings Limited has sold 6,336,500 A shares in Nanjing Canatal, representing about 1.17% of the latter’s issued share capital, through open market bidding and block trades between 25 March and 8 April 2026. The disposal generated approximately RMB61.5 million in cash proceeds and reduced Analogue’s stake in the Shanghai-listed precision environmental control specialist from around 15.70% to 14.53%, qualifying as a discloseable but shareholder-approval-exempt transaction under Hong Kong listing rules.

China International Development Corporation Limited has announced that its planned placing of up to 25 million new ordinary shares under a general mandate will not proceed, after conditions precedent under the placing agreement failed to be satisfied by the closing date. Pizu Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:9893) ) has issued an update.

Pizu Group Holdings Limited has called an extraordinary general meeting in Hong Kong on 28 April 2026 for shareholders to vote on a proposal to change the company’s domicile from the Cayman Islands to Hong Kong. The move, subject to regulatory approvals and the issuance of a re-domiciliation certificate, would see the company adopt new articles of association compliant with Hong Kong’s Companies Ordinance, potentially aligning its corporate governance and regulatory oversight more closely with its primary market and stakeholder base.

The board is seeking shareholder authorization to implement all necessary steps and filings in both jurisdictions to complete the re-domiciliation and related corporate changes. Shareholders are being reminded of their rights to attend, vote, or appoint proxies for the meeting, underscoring the significance of the proposed shift in legal domicile for corporate structure, regulatory regime, and long-term alignment with Hong Kong’s legal framework.

More about Pizu Group Holdings Limited

Pizu Group Holdings Limited is a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong. It operates under a board led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Ma Tianyi and maintains a principal office in Hong Kong, reflecting its operational and governance ties to the territory despite its current Cayman domicile.

data.title: Kafelaku Coffee Sets Audit Committee Mandate to Bolster Financial Oversight
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Li Bao Ge Group Ltd. ( (HK:1869) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Kafelaku Coffee Holding Limited has formally established an audit committee of its board of directors, composed mainly of independent non-executive directors, to enhance oversight of its financial reporting, risk management, and internal control systems. The committee must have at least three non-executive directors, with at least one possessing professional accounting or financial management expertise, and its chair must be an independent non-executive director.

The newly defined terms of reference grant the audit committee authority to manage the relationship with external auditors, including recommending appointments, removals, and remuneration, and to review auditor independence and audit effectiveness. It is also empowered to demand information from group employees, investigate potential improprieties in financial reporting or internal controls, and seek external legal or professional advice, signaling a stronger governance framework for shareholders and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1869) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.13 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Li Bao Ge Group Ltd. stock, see the HK:1869 Stock Forecast page.

More about Li Bao Ge Group Ltd.

Kafelaku Coffee Holding Limited, listed in Hong Kong and incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operates in the food and beverage sector with a focus on coffee-related businesses. The company is governed by a board of directors that includes non-executive and independent non-executive members in line with Hong Kong Listing Rules requirements.

The group structure includes various subsidiaries, and its governance framework emphasizes compliance with financial reporting, risk management, and internal control standards. By formalizing committees such as the audit committee, Kafelaku Coffee aims to strengthen oversight of its accounting practices and external auditing arrangements.

data.description:

An announcement from Bank of Tianjin Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1578) ) is now available.

Bank of Tianjin Co., Ltd., a PRC-incorporated joint stock commercial bank, provides banking and financial services primarily in mainland China and does not hold authorization to operate as a banking institution in Hong Kong.

The bank has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for 29 April 2026 in Tianjin, where shareholders will vote on the 2025 profit distribution plan, the board’s work report, the 2025 annual report and the appointment of external auditors for 2026, while also receiving performance appraisals and reports on independent directors and related-party transactions, underscoring governance, transparency and oversight of its management and financial practices.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1578) stock is a Hold with a HK$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bank of Tianjin Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:1578 Stock Forecast page.

More about Bank of Tianjin Co., Ltd. Class H

Bank of Tianjin Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China that operates as a commercial bank. It focuses on providing banking and financial services in mainland China and is not an authorised institution under Hong Kong’s Banking Ordinance, meaning it is not supervised by the Hong Kong Monetary Authority and cannot conduct banking or deposit-taking business in Hong Kong.
